title: stelis aemula
intro: Stelis aemula, also known as the false orchid, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the orchid family and is native to Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Stelis aemula include its ability to treat skin conditions such as acne, eczema, and wounds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. Stelis aemula can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
